title: Astracantha zoharyi
taxonomy: Astracantha zoharyi is classified as a species within the genus Astracantha, which is a part of the family Fabaceae, also known as the legume family. The genus Astracantha is a group of thorny shrubs native to the Mediterranean region. Astracantha zoharyi, specifically, is a thorny shrub that is found in Israel. It is often found in dry areas and is known for its ability to withstand harsh conditions. Astracantha zoharyi is often used in landscaping due to its unique appearance and ability to thrive in dry environments.
uses: Astracantha zoharyi is a species of spiny shrub native to the Middle East, but little information is available about its specific medicinal uses. However, it is part of the Astragalus genus, which has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Some Astragalus species are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and inflammation. These species often contain bioactive compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Further research is needed to determine the specific medicinal uses of Astracantha zoharyi.
benefits: Astracantha zoharyi is known for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever and digestive issues. Its extracts have been studied for their ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, making it a potential natural remedy for infections. Additionally, Astracantha zoharyi has been shown to have antidiabetic and antihypertensive effects, suggesting its potential in managing chronic diseases. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.

constituents: Astracantha zoharyi contains active constituents such as flavonoids and alkaloids, which may provide various health benefits. preparations: Astracantha zoharyi, a plant native to the Middle East, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. side_effects: Astracantha zoharyi is a plant that may cause irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin, due to the presence of certain compounds. Skin contact with the plant's sap or leaves can lead to redness, itching, and swelling. Ingesting any part of the plant can cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap or leaves may also cause photosensitivity, increasing the risk of sunburn or skin damage upon exposure to sunlight. It is essential to exercise caution when handling the plant to avoid any potential side effects.
